events.report: Female, approximately 35, trapped under concrete slab with chest pain and difficulty breathing. Resp rate 24, radial pulse strong, GCS 14.
Male, approximately 42, found pulseless and not breathing after extraction. No purposeful movement. GCS 3.
Male, approximately 28, walking wounded with forehead laceration and ankle pain. Resp rate 18, radial pulse strong, GCS 15.
Female, approximately 51, severe leg deformity, weak radial pulse, resp rate 32, GCS 13.
Male, approximately 60, trapped with crush injury to pelvis, resp rate 28, radial pulse weak, GCS 12.
Female, approximately 44, open humerus fracture, resp rate 22, radial pulse present, GCS 15.
Male, approximately 40, found under rubble, compound fracture left femur, GCS 12, resp rate 28, radial pulse weak.
Female, approximately 19, ambulatory with abrasions and crying. Resp rate 20, radial pulse strong, GCS 15.
Male, approximately 33, shortness of breath and chest trauma, resp rate 34, radial pulse present, GCS 14.
Female, approximately 70, confused, scalp laceration, resp rate 26, radial pulse strong, GCS 11.
Male, approximately 49, abdominal pain with bruising, resp rate 24, radial pulse present, GCS 15.
Female, approximately 25, walking and carrying another victim, mild wrist pain, resp rate 16, radial pulse strong, GCS 15.
Male, approximately 58, facial burns and hoarse voice, resp rate 30, radial pulse present, GCS 14.
Female, approximately 31, walking wounded with knee pain, resp rate 18, radial pulse strong, GCS 15.
Male, approximately 24, superficial cuts and anxiety, walking. Resp rate 18, radial pulse strong, GCS 15.
Female, approximately 46, shoulder pain, walking, resp rate 18, radial pulse strong, GCS 15.
Male, approximately 54, laceration to forearm, walking, resp rate 19, radial pulse strong, GCS 15.
Female, approximately 38, minor limp and abrasions, walking, resp rate 17, radial pulse strong, GCS 15.
Male, approximately 47, dizziness but walking, resp rate 20, radial pulse strong, GCS 15.
Female, approximately 36, new collapse victim, decreased breath sounds, resp rate 36, radial pulse weak, GCS 12.
Male, approximately 61, crush chest injury, resp rate 34, radial pulse weak, GCS 11.
Female, approximately 29, entrapped with bilateral leg fractures, resp rate 29, radial pulse weak, GCS 13.
Male, approximately 52, burn injury to arm and face, soot around mouth, resp rate 31, radial pulse present, GCS 14.
Patient P-001 worsening, now more short of breath. Resp rate 32, radial pulse weak, GCS 13.
Patient P-011 now hypotensive and confused. Resp rate 30, radial pulse weak, GCS 12.
Female, approximately 66, pelvic pain and tenderness, resp rate 24, radial pulse present, GCS 14.
Male, approximately 23, open tib-fib fracture, resp rate 23, radial pulse present, GCS 15.
